steps.title: Identify the setup and the transition
steps.content: The passage explains that river irrigation worked so well the Sumerians often harvested "even more crops than they needed in a season." The blank follows "As a result," signaling the logical consequence of having a surplus.

steps.title: Derive the consequence
steps.content: If a farming community regularly produces more than it needs, the natural next step is to keep the extra for later. The completion should describe what they did with the surplus.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — harvested crops only on the hottest days of each season.: overreach — the passage never says anything about timing harvests by temperature, and the hot weather is described as a problem, not a harvest schedule. ✗
  • Choice B — found ways to shield their crops from the sun.: misread — the drying-out problem was already solved by river irrigation; the "As a result" here follows the surplus, not the sun problem. ✗
  • Choice C — did not begin farming until long after 4000 BCE.: contradicts the passage, which dates Sumerian farming to around 4000 BCE. ✗
  • Choice D — were able to store extra crops for later use.: matches the surplus — storing extras is the direct consequence of harvesting more than needed. ✓
That is the correct answer.
